files.title: AlignOrientation
files.description: The AlignOrientation constraint applies torque to align two attachments, or to align one attachment with a goal orientation.

files.title: AlignPosition
files.description: The AlignPosition constraint applies force to move two attachments together, or to move one attachment to a goal position.

files.title: AngularVelocity
files.description: The AngularVelocity constraint applies torque on an assembly to maintain a constant angular velocity.

files.title: AnimationConstraint
files.description: AnimationConstraint aligns two base parts with an animate-able kinematic or force-based joint.

files.title: BallSocket
files.description: BallSocketConstraint forces its two attachments into the same position and allows them to freely rotate about all three axes, with optional limits to restrict both tilt and twist.

files.title: Cylindrical
files.description: CylindricalConstraint allows its attachments to slide along one axis and rotate about another axis, with optional assigned angular and/or linear power.

files.title: Hinge
files.description: HingeConstraint allows its two attachments to rotate about one axis, with optional assigned power for motor or servo behavior.

files.title: LineForce
files.description: The LineForce constraint applies force along the theoretical line connecting its two attachments.

files.title: LinearVelocity
files.description: The LinearVelocity constraint applies force on an assembly to maintain a constant velocity along a 3D vector, line, or 2D plane.

files.title: NoCollision
files.description: NoCollisionConstraint prevents collisions between two specific parts, but those parts may still register collisions with the rest of the world.

files.title: Plane
files.description: PlaneConstraint moves two attachments into a position/orientation along a plane, and both attachments remain free to translate and rotate unless otherwise constrained.

files.title: Prismatic
files.description: PrismaticConstraint allows two attachments to slide along one axis but not rotate, with optional assigned power for mechanisms like sliding doors and elevator platforms.

files.title: Rigid
files.description: RigidConstraint connects two attachments with zero offset.

files.title: Rod
files.description: RodConstraint keeps two attachments separated by a defined length, with optional limits on rotational tilt.

files.title: Rope
files.description: RopeConstraint prevents two attachments from separating further than a defined length, with optional behavior as an extending or contracting winch.

files.title: Spring
files.description: SpringConstraint applies a force on its attachments based on spring and damper behavior, with an optional minimum/maximum length.

files.title: Torque
files.description: The Torque constraint applies constant torque on an assembly from its center of mass.

files.title: TorsionSpring
files.description: TorsionSpringConstraint applies torque based on a relative angle and relative angular velocity, in an attempt to bring two axes from two parts together.

files.title: Universal
files.description: UniversalConstraint ensures two axes on two assemblies remain perpendicular, useful for applications such as vehicle power transmission to rear drive shafts, robotics, and more.

files.title: VectorForce
files.description: The VectorForce constraint applies constant linear force on an assembly.

files.title: Weld
files.description: WeldConstraint attaches two BaseParts together and ensures they stay in the same relative position and orientation to each other.
